questions.q: In which year was the actor Naseeruddin Shah born?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah was born on 20 July 1949 in Barabanki, Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: From which renowned film institute did Naseeruddin Shah graduate?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah trained at the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII) in Pune.

questions.q: Naseeruddin Shah became a leading face of which movement in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah was one of the foremost actors of the Parallel cinema (New Indian Cinema) movement.

questions.q: Which 1975 Shyam Benegal film was an early landmark in Naseeruddin Shah's career?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah appeared in Shyam Benegal's Nishant (1975), an early film of his Parallel cinema career.

questions.q: In the 1980 film Sparsh, Naseeruddin Shah played a man who is which of the following?
questions.explanation: In Sai Paranjpye's Sparsh (1980), Naseeruddin Shah played a blind man who runs a school for blind boys.

questions.q: Which theatre group did Naseeruddin Shah co-found?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah co-founded the theatre group Motley in 1977, along with Benjamin Gilani and Tom Alter.

questions.q: How many National Film Awards has Naseeruddin Shah won?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah is a three-time National Film Award winner (Best Actor for Sparsh and Paar, and Best Supporting Actor for Iqbal).

questions.q: In which 1983 Shyam Benegal ensemble film about a brothel did Naseeruddin Shah act?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah appeared in Shyam Benegal's Mandi (1983), a satire set in a brothel, where he played Tungrus.

questions.q: Which 1980 Govind Nihalani film featuring Naseeruddin Shah dealt with caste oppression and a silent tribal accused of murder?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah starred in Govind Nihalani's debut feature Aakrosh (1980), a courtroom drama about caste injustice.

questions.q: Naseeruddin Shah is best described as which of the following?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah is one of Indian cinema's most acclaimed actors, equally active in art and mainstream films.

questions.q: For which Sai Paranjpye film, in which he plays a blind school principal, did Naseeruddin Shah win his first National Film Award for Best Actor?
options: Albert Pinto Ko Gussa Kyoon Aata Hai
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah won his first National Film Award for Best Actor for Sai Paranjpye's Sparsh.

questions.q: In which 1983 Shekhar Kapur film did Naseeruddin Shah play a married man confronted by his illegitimate son, opposite Shabana Azmi?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah played the lead in Shekhar Kapur's directorial debut Masoom (1983), with Shabana Azmi as his wife.

questions.q: Which Sai Paranjpye comedy, an urban retelling of the tortoise-and-hare fable, starred Naseeruddin Shah alongside Farooq Shaikh and Deepti Naval?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah played the honest tenant Rajaram in Sai Paranjpye's Katha (1983), a comedy based on the tortoise-and-hare fable.

questions.q: In which Ketan Mehta film, drawing on Gujarati folk theatre, did Naseeruddin Shah appear in a caste-based satire told in the Bhavai performance style?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah appeared in Ketan Mehta's debut Bhavni Bhavai (1980), a satire told in the Gujarati Bhavai folk-theatre style.

questions.q: For which 2005 Nagesh Kukunoor film, in which he played an alcoholic former cricketer coaching a deaf-mute boy, did Naseeruddin Shah win the National Film Award for Best Supporting Actor?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah played the washed-up coach Mohit in Nagesh Kukunoor's Iqbal (2005), winning the National Film Award for Best Supporting Actor.

questions.q: In which acclaimed 1983 Govind Nihalani police drama did Naseeruddin Shah appear opposite Om Puri's lead role?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah had a role in Govind Nihalani's Ardh Satya (1983), which starred Om Puri as the conflicted police officer.

questions.q: Which 2006 Rajkumar Hirani comedy featured Naseeruddin Shah in a cameo as Mahatma Gandhi?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah played Mahatma Gandhi in Rajkumar Hirani's Lage Raho Munna Bhai (2006).

questions.q: Naseeruddin Shah played the submarine captain Captain Nemo in which 2003 Hollywood adventure film?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah played Captain Nemo in the Hollywood film The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen (2003).

questions.q: Which civilian honour did the Government of India award Naseeruddin Shah in 1987?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah was awarded the Padma Shri in 1987 and later the Padma Bhushan in 2003.

questions.q: In the 2003 film Maqbool, an adaptation of Shakespeare's Macbeth, what kind of character did Naseeruddin Shah play?
questions.explanation: In Vishal Bhardwaj's Maqbool (2003), Naseeruddin Shah played one of the two corrupt policemen who function as the witches of Macbeth.

questions.q: Which 1976 Shyam Benegal film about a cooperative milk movement in Gujarat featured Naseeruddin Shah?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah appeared in Shyam Benegal's Manthan (1976), inspired by the Operation Flood dairy cooperative movement.

questions.q: What is the title of Naseeruddin Shah's 2014 autobiography covering his early life and career?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah's memoir, titled And Then One Day, was published in 2014.

questions.q: Naseeruddin Shah's wife, a noted actress who appeared with him in many Parallel cinema films, is named whom?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah is married to actress Ratna Pathak Shah, whom he wed in 1982.

questions.q: In Gulzar's acclaimed late-1980s television series, Naseeruddin Shah played the title role of which 19th-century Urdu poet?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah played the poet Mirza Ghalib in Gulzar's 1988 television serial Mirza Ghalib.

questions.q: In which 1980 Saeed Akhtar Mirza film did Naseeruddin Shah play an angry young mechanic, the title character?
options: Albert Pinto Ko Gussa Kyoon Aata Hai
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah played the title role in Saeed Akhtar Mirza's Albert Pinto Ko Gussa Kyoon Aata Hai (1980).

questions.q: Naseeruddin Shah made his directorial film debut in 2006 with which movie?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah directed the 2006 film Yun Hota Toh Kya Hota, his only feature as director.

questions.q: In Neeraj Pandey's 2008 thriller A Wednesday!, Naseeruddin Shah played which character?
questions.explanation: In A Wednesday! (2008), Naseeruddin Shah played an unnamed 'common man' who confronts the police over terrorism.

questions.q: Which 1978 Shyam Benegal historical film, set during the 1857 revolt, starred Naseeruddin Shah and was produced by Shashi Kapoor?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah appeared in Junoon (1978), Shyam Benegal's film set during the Sepoy Mutiny of 1857, produced by Shashi Kapoor.

questions.q: In which 2011 film about the South Indian actress Silk Smitha did Naseeruddin Shah play an ageing film superstar?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah played the ageing star Suryakant in The Dirty Picture (2011), inspired by the life of Silk Smitha.

questions.q: In the 2010 dark comedy Ishqiya, Naseeruddin Shah co-starred with which actor as a pair of thieves?
questions.explanation: In Ishqiya (2010), Naseeruddin Shah played Khalujaan opposite Arshad Warsi as his nephew Babban.
